description: Eval cases for the InkBloop multi-agent system. Tests AI parsing, entity resolution, and end-to-end outcomes.

evals.context: One client named Sarah Kim exists
evals.resolution: Exact single match on 'sarah' → resolves to Sarah Kim
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens pre-filled with client=Sarah Kim, date=next Friday 2pm

evals.prompt: create an appointment for Sam this saturday at 1 pm
evals.context: Two clients match 'Sam': Sam Donovan and Samuel Wright
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'sam' → shows client disambiguation cards for Sam Donovan and Samuel Wright
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Which Sam?' with two client cards. After tap, BookingForm opens with selected client + Saturday 1pm

evals.context: No client named Sandy exists. Compound flow: tapping Create New Client should preserve the booking intent and resume into BookingForm after client is created.
evals.resolution: No match on 'sandy' → shows no-match UI with 'Create new client' + fuzzy suggestions. Booking intent is stashed in pendingIntent.
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'No exact match for sandy' with Create New Client button. Tapping it opens CreateClientForm with name pre-filled. After save, AppShell.onCreated fires and resumes agent flow → BookingForm opens with the new client_id + original date=next tuesday. Zero re-typing.

evals.prompt: create an appt for cindy lou
evals.context: Client 'Cindy Liu' exists (voice-to-text error: 'lou' vs 'liu')
evals.resolution: No exact match on 'cindy lou' → fuzzy match finds 'Cindy Liu' as top suggestion
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows no-match UI with Cindy Liu as a smart suggestion

evals.prompt: book a consultation for friday morning
evals.context: No client name mentioned in prompt
evals.resolution: No client_name in entities → skip client resolution, proceed directly
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Consultation, date=Friday, timeSlot=morning, no client pre-filled

evals.prompt: book chris martin for a full day session next wednesday, estimate 800 dollars, sleeve work on left arm
evals.context: Client Chris Martin exists (exact full name match)
evals.resolution: Exact match on 'chris martin' → resolves immediately
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with all fields pre-filled: client, date, type, estimate, notes

evals.context: Testing shorthand date parsing: '52' = May 2nd
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves to Sarah Kim
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with client=Sarah Kim, date=May 2 at 3pm

evals.prompt: block off tomorrow at 9am for a dentist appointment
evals.context: Personal calendar block — single-word title, no client involved
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution entirely
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title=Dentist (shown instead of client picker), date=tomorrow 9am, duration defaults to 1h

evals.prompt: block off tomorrow morning at 7 for the gym
evals.context: Personal booking for a fitness block
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution, 1-hour duration default
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title=Gym, date=tomorrow 7am, duration=1h

evals.prompt: make a personal appointment for the doctor next tuesday at 10
evals.context: Explicit 'personal' keyword + a trigger noun (doctor)
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title=Doctor, date=next tuesday 10am

evals.prompt: block off 2 hours tomorrow afternoon for errands
evals.context: Personal booking with explicit duration override (not the 1-hour default). timeSlot inference from 'afternoon' is not a Personal-specific concern, so we don't assert it here.
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. duration honored.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title=Errands, duration=2h, date=tomorrow

evals.prompt: personal appointment friday at noon - lunch with mom at the italian place downtown
evals.context: Title is flexible wording (Lunch / Lunch w Mom / Lunch with Mom) so we don't assert an exact title string; the critical check is type=Personal and NO client_name (mom isn't a client)
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. 'mom' must NOT be treated as a client_name; title holds the personal label.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title populated (short), notes populated with overflow, no client linked

evals.prompt: block off sunday at 6pm to have dinner with mom
evals.context: Regression guard: 'mom' is NOT one of our clients and must not be leaked into client_name. This is the scenario that broke pre-Personal: a relative name in a personal booking getting resolved against the client list.
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution entirely. Critical: parsed entities must NOT contain client_name.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, no client linked, title populated, date=Sunday 6pm

evals.context: Sarah Kim has 2 upcoming bookings
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves client → finds 2 bookings → shows booking cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Which booking?' with booking cards for Sarah's upcoming appointments

evals.context: Alex Torres has exactly 1 upcoming booking
evals.resolution: Single match on 'alex' → resolves client → finds exactly 1 booking → auto-opens
evals.expected_outcome: BookingDrawer opens directly showing Alex's booking details

evals.context: One booking exists on Friday
evals.resolution: No client name → skip client resolution → filter bookings by date → one match → auto-opens
evals.expected_outcome: BookingDrawer opens for the Friday booking

evals.prompt: do I have an appointment for cindy
evals.context: Two clients match 'cindy'. Must NOT route to client agent.
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'cindy' → shows client disambiguation cards → after tap, shows booking cards (never auto-opens)
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Which cindy?' with client cards. After tap, shows booking(s) as cards or 'No upcoming bookings found for cindy.'

evals.context: 3 bookings exist on Friday
evals.resolution: No client → filter bookings by date → show all as cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Found 3 bookings:' with booking cards

evals.context: Sarah Kim has 1 booking this week
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → finds 1 booking → shows as card
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Found 1 booking for sarah:' with booking card

evals.prompt: reschedule sarah to next monday at 4pm
evals.context: Sarah Kim has 1 upcoming booking
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves client → finds booking → opens edit form
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens in edit mode with date changed to Monday 4pm, rescheduled=true, fields highlighted as 'AI updated'

evals.prompt: change the estimate on chris's booking to 500
evals.context: Chris Martin has 1 upcoming booking. Christina Reyes also matches 'chris'.
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'chris' (Chris Martin, Christina Reyes) → shows client disambiguation cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Which chris?' with two client cards. After tap, BookingForm opens with estimate=500 highlighted

evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both exist
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'chris' → disambiguation → then find booking → edit
evals.expected_outcome: Disambiguation first, then BookingForm opens with time changed to 3pm

evals.prompt: add a new client named Taylor Swift
evals.context: No client named Taylor Swift exists
evals.resolution: No resolution needed for create
evals.expected_outcome: CreateClientForm opens with name pre-filled as 'Taylor Swift'

evals.context: No client named Mike Johnson exists
evals.resolution: No resolution needed for create
evals.expected_outcome: CreateClientForm opens with name='Mike Johnson' and phone='555-9999' pre-filled

evals.prompt: do I have a client named cindy
evals.context: Client Cindy Liu exists
evals.resolution: Single match on 'cindy' → shows as search result card (does NOT auto-navigate)
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Found a match for cindy:' with Cindy Liu card, Create New button, and search field

evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both match
evals.resolution: Multiple matches → shows all as search result cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Found 2 clients matching chris:' with both client cards

evals.prompt: do I have a client named rodriguez
evals.context: No client matches 'rodriguez'
evals.resolution: No match → shows no-match UI with fuzzy suggestions
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows no-match UI with Create New button and any fuzzy suggestions

evals.context: 'Sara' doesn't exactly match anyone, but 'Sarah Kim' is close (edit distance 1)
evals.resolution: No exact match → fuzzy match finds 'Sarah Kim' as suggestion
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows no-match with Sarah Kim as a smart suggestion

evals.context: Josh Parrott exists as exact single match
evals.resolution: Single match on 'josh' → navigates directly
evals.expected_outcome: Panel closes, app navigates to Josh Parrott's client profile page

evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both match 'chris'
evals.resolution: Multiple matches → disambiguation cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Which chris?' with two client cards. Tapping one navigates to that profile.

evals.context: Sarah Kim exists, single match
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves client → second AI call with current data + prompt → computes { phone: '555-1234' }
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with phone field showing '555-1234', highlighted with accent border and 'AI updated' label

evals.prompt: update josh's last name to have two t's
evals.context: Josh Parrott exists. Current name: 'Josh Parrott'. User wants 'Josh Parrottt'? Or 'Josh Parrott' already has two t's? Depends on actual data.
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves to Josh Parrott → second AI call gets current name + user request → AI computes new name value
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with name field updated to AI-computed value, highlighted as 'AI updated'

evals.context: Alex Torres exists with tags=['new']
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves to Alex Torres → second AI call sees current tags=['new'] + 'add VIP' → returns tags=['new', 'VIP']
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with tags='new, VIP', highlighted as 'AI updated'

evals.prompt: remove the returning tag from josh
evals.context: Josh Parrott has tags=['returning', 'cover-up']
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves → second AI call sees current tags + removal request → returns tags=['cover-up']
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with tags='cover-up', highlighted as 'AI updated'

evals.context: 5 bookings exist this week, 3 are Regular type
evals.resolution: No entity resolution needed → computes from bookingStore
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'You have 5 bookings this week' with booking cards. Panel stays open.

evals.prompt: how many consultations do I have this month
evals.context: 2 Consultation bookings exist this month
evals.resolution: Filters by booking_type=Consultation
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'You have 2 consultations this month'

evals.context: 3 bookings exist on Friday
evals.resolution: Filters bookings to Friday → returns list
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows '3 bookings on Friday:' with booking cards

evals.context: Working hours 10-8, some days have gaps >= 2 hours
evals.resolution: Checks each working day for gaps >= minGapForAvailable
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows available days as a bullet list

evals.prompt: give me a summary of this week
evals.context: Mix of booking types this week
evals.resolution: Aggregates bookings by type with counts and hours
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows breakdown: Regular: 3 (9h), Touch Up: 1 (2h), etc. with totals

evals.context: 2 bookings exist tomorrow
evals.resolution: Filters to tomorrow
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows '2 bookings tomorrow:' with booking cards

evals.context: Alex Torres has 1 conversation thread (Instagram only)
evals.resolution: Single match on 'alex' → resolves client → 1 conversation thread → auto-opens
evals.expected_outcome: ConversationDrawer opens showing Alex's Instagram thread

evals.context: Cindy Liu has both Instagram and Facebook conversation threads
evals.resolution: Single match on 'cindy' → resolves client → 2 conversation threads → shows platform cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'Cindy Liu has multiple message threads' with Instagram and Messenger cards

evals.context: Sarah Kim has no linked Instagram or Facebook PSIDs
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves client → no conversation threads found
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'No conversation thread found for Sarah Kim'

evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both match. Chris has IG thread.
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'chris' → disambiguation → resolves client → finds thread → shows template cards
evals.expected_outcome: After client disambiguation, panel shows template cards (Reminder, Follow-up, Reschedule). Tapping one opens ConversationDrawer with draft.

evals.context: Alex Torres has 1 IG thread
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves client → finds thread → shows template cards
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows template cards. Tapping one opens ConversationDrawer with draft text pre-filled.

evals.context: Sam Donovan has an appointment on Wednesday. Thursday is a different day — the date is the TARGET, not the current booking date.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sam' → resolves to Sam Donovan → finds booking by client (NOT by target date) → edit form opens with new date + rescheduled flag
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens in edit mode with date changed to Thursday, rescheduled=true, both highlighted as 'AI updated'

evals.prompt: move sarah's booking to next wednesday
evals.context: Sarah Kim has a booking on Monday. 'move' implies reschedule.
evals.resolution: Single match → finds booking by client → edit with new date
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with date changed to Wednesday, rescheduled=true

evals.context: Today IS Thursday. 'Thursday' should mean NEXT Thursday (7 days from now), not today. Only 'today' means today.

entities.date: next thursday ISO (7 days from now)

evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → BookingForm opens with date = next Thursday
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with date set to next Thursday (7 days from now), NOT today

evals.context: Today is Thursday. 'Friday' should mean tomorrow (1 day from now), not 8 days from now.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → BookingForm opens with date = tomorrow Friday
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with date set to tomorrow (Friday), the next strictly-future occurrence

evals.context: The word 'today' is the only way to mean today's date.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → BookingForm opens with date = today at 2pm
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with date set to today at 2pm

evals.context: 'tomorrow' should always mean the next calendar day.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'alex' → BookingForm opens with date = tomorrow, timeSlot = morning
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with date = tomorrow, time slot = morning

evals.context: Nonsensical input
evals.resolution: Unknown agent → no resolution
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'I'm not sure what you'd like to do. Try something like...'

evals.context: Request outside app's capabilities
evals.resolution: Unknown agent → no resolution
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows 'I'm not sure what you'd like to do. Try something like...'

evals.prompt: book an appointment for kris martin
evals.context: Client is 'Chris Martin' — voice-to-text used 'kris' instead of 'chris'
evals.resolution: No exact match on 'kris martin' → fuzzy match scores 'Chris Martin' high (first name edit distance 1, last name exact) → shows as suggestion
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows no-match UI with Chris Martin as top suggestion

evals.context: Client is 'Christina Reyes' — phonetic mismatch
evals.resolution: No exact match → fuzzy match finds 'Christina Reyes' (edit distance 1 on first name) → suggestion
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows no-match UI with Christina Reyes as suggestion

evals.prompt: I need to see sarah next thursday in the afternoon
evals.context: Sarah Kim exists, single match
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves → opens booking form
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with client=Sarah Kim, date=next Thursday, timeSlot=evening (afternoon maps to evening)

evals.prompt: am I booked up on friday
evals.context: 4 bookings on Friday filling most of the day
evals.resolution: Checks Friday for availability
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows whether Friday has available slots or not

evals.prompt: push sarah's appointment to next week
evals.context: Sarah Kim has 1 upcoming booking
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves client → finds booking → but 'next week' is vague, date might not resolve cleanly
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens in edit mode with rescheduled=true. Date handling depends on how AI parses 'next week'.

evals.prompt: book a touch up for chris next monday at 10am, 2 hours, estimate 200, forearm piece
evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both match
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'chris' → disambiguation first, then BookingForm with all fields
evals.expected_outcome: After disambiguation, BookingForm opens with ALL entities pre-filled: type, date, duration, estimate, notes

evals.context: Ambiguous between search and open — should be 'open' (user assumes client exists)
evals.resolution: Multiple matches → disambiguation → navigates to profile
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows client disambiguation, then navigates to selected client's profile

evals.prompt: is there a client called martinez
evals.context: Phrased as a question — should be 'search' not 'open'
evals.resolution: Search → shows results as cards, never auto-navigates
evals.expected_outcome: Agent panel shows search results (or no-match with suggestions)

evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both match 'chris'. One of them has a Friday booking.
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'chris' → disambiguation → filter bookings by client+date → one match → confirm_delete_booking card
evals.expected_outcome: After disambiguation, panel shows a confirm card with red 'Delete' and neutral 'Cancel' buttons. Tapping Delete removes the booking via bookingStore.deleteBooking and shows a toast.

evals.context: Sarah Kim has 1 upcoming booking. 'cancel' is a delete synonym.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves client → finds 1 booking → confirm_delete_booking card
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows confirm card. Tapping Delete removes Sarah's booking and shows a toast.

evals.context: One booking exists on Friday. No client name — filter by date only.
evals.resolution: No client → filter bookings by date → one match → confirm_delete_booking card
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows confirm card for the Friday booking. Tapping Delete removes it.

evals.context: Alex Torres has 2 upcoming bookings.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'alex' → finds 2 bookings → shows booking cards for selection → after tap, confirm_delete_booking card
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows booking cards. After tap, confirm card appears. Tapping Delete removes the selected booking.

evals.context: Sarah Kim exists, single match.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves client → confirm_delete_client card
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows 'Delete Sarah Kim?' confirm card with warning that all of her bookings will also be removed. Tapping Delete calls clientStore.deleteClient.

evals.context: Chris Martin and Christina Reyes both match 'chris'.
evals.resolution: Multiple matches on 'chris' → disambiguation cards → after tap, confirm_delete_client card
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows 'Which chris?' with two client cards. After tap, confirm card appears.

evals.context: No client named Sandy exists. Can't delete a non-existent client.
evals.resolution: No match on 'sandy' → shows no-match UI (no confirm card)
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows 'No exact match for sandy' with fuzzy suggestions. No confirm card since no client was resolved.

evals.context: Sarah Kim exists, single match. Full ISO date provided.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'sarah' → resolves client → agent-parse extracts dob directly in first pass. Second agent-resolve-edit call may refine but isn't required for direct-value edits.
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with dob set to April 15, 1990, highlighted as 'AI updated'. User reviews and saves.

evals.prompt: update chris martin's birthday to april 15
evals.context: Chris Martin exists, exact match on full name. Month+day only — year must be inferred.
evals.resolution: Exact match → resolves client → agent-parse extracts dob with any reasonable year (runner checks presence only, not exact year).
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with dob set to April 15 (some year), highlighted as 'AI updated'. User can edit the year if needed.

evals.context: Alex Torres exists, single match. 'bday' is shorthand for birthday/dob.
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves client → agent-parse extracts dob from 'bday' synonym → returns YYYY-05-02 (year runner-skipped).
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with dob set to May 2, highlighted as 'AI updated'.

evals.prompt: josh's date of birth is july 20 1985
evals.context: Josh Parrott exists. 'date of birth' is a dob synonym. Full date provided.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'josh' → resolves → agent-parse extracts dob=1985-07-20.
evals.expected_outcome: ClientForm opens with dob set to July 20, 1985, highlighted as 'AI updated'.

evals.prompt: book chris martin in the first available morning slot
evals.context: Chris Martin exists, exact match on full name. No date given — orchestrator should compute next open morning slot.
evals.resolution: Exact match → resolves client → orchestrator calls findFirstAvailableSlot('morning') which iterates up to 60 days forward using scheduleConfig → sets date to computed ISO → BookingForm opens.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with client=Chris Martin, date=next open morning slot (computed client-side), timeSlot=morning.

evals.context: Sarah Kim exists, single match. No date — orchestrator finds next open evening slot.
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves → orchestrator calls findFirstAvailableSlot('evening') → BookingForm opens with computed date.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with client=Sarah Kim, date=next open evening slot, timeSlot=evening.

evals.context: Alex Torres exists. No date or time preference — any open slot.
evals.resolution: Single match → resolves → orchestrator calls findFirstAvailableSlot('any') → sets date to first open slot.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with client=Alex Torres, date=first open slot of any kind.

evals.prompt: book josh for his next available appointment
evals.context: Josh Parrott exists. 'next available' without time preference → find_slot='any'.
evals.resolution: Single match on 'josh' → resolves → findFirstAvailableSlot('any') → BookingForm opens.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with client=Josh Parrott, date=first available open slot.

evals.context: Explicit date given — find_slot must NOT be set (date takes precedence).
evals.resolution: Explicit date → agent-parse should NOT set find_slot. If find_slot slips through, orchestrator preserves the explicit date anyway.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with date=Friday 2pm. No slot-finding logic runs.

evals.prompt: Create Levi as a client and then schedule a touchup for him next Sunday
evals.context: Compound prompt: create a new client AND book an appointment in one shot. No client named Levi exists. Must parse as booking/create (not client/create) so the no-match flow can chain through to the booking form.
evals.resolution: agent-parse returns booking/create with client_name=Levi + booking entities. Orchestrator resolves Levi → none → showNoMatch with 'Create new client' hint. User taps → CreateClientForm opens with name=Levi. Save → onCreated fires → agentHandleSelection('client', newClientId) → routeBooking resumes with the stashed booking entities intact → BookingForm opens with client=Levi, type=Touch Up, date=next Sunday. Safety net: if AI returns client/create, orchestrator's hasBookingIntent() check rewrites it to booking/create client-side.
evals.expected_outcome: Zero-retyping compound flow: one prompt → one tap on 'Create new client' → one save on CreateClientForm → BookingForm opens with everything filled.

evals.prompt: add a new client Sam and book him Friday 2pm
evals.context: Compound prompt variant. Sam might ambiguously match existing Sam Donovan / Samuel Wright, but the explicit 'add a new client' phrasing signals a new client. Must parse as booking/create so the new-client flow happens.
evals.resolution: agent-parse returns booking/create with client_name=Sam. resolveClient('Sam') → multiple matches → disambiguation cards. User either picks an existing Sam or (better UX) sees 'Create new client Sam' option. Current implementation shows disambiguation first.
evals.expected_outcome: Panel shows 'Which Sam?' disambiguation. After selecting or creating, BookingForm opens with Friday 2pm.

evals.prompt: block off may 5 for my surgery
evals.context: Single-day all-day blocking event. Artist-first phrasing ('my surgery') → artist unavailable.
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. is_all_day=true with end_date=midnight of next day (exclusive-end). blocks_availability=true because the artist themself is the subject.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title=Surgery, is_all_day=true, blocks_availability=true, date=may 5, end_date=may 6 00:00 (exclusive). Schedule agent will count this day as unavailable.

evals.context: Multi-day all-day blocking event. 'Vacation' + 'block off' + 'for' implies the artist is unavailable across the whole range.
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. end_date=midnight AFTER the last covered day (may 15 00:00 for a range ending may 14, exclusive-end convention). blocks_availability=true because it's the artist's own vacation.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with an all-day bar spanning may 10-14 inclusive. Schedule agent treats all 5 days as unavailable.

evals.prompt: john is in town from jun 7 to 9
evals.context: Third-party informational event: someone ELSE is in town. Artist remains free to book. Default for all-day ambiguity leans to false but 'in town' is a strong non-blocking cue.
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution entirely. 'john' is NOT a client_name (informational note, not a tattoo booking). Title should contain 'John' (e.g. 'John in town'). blocks_availability=false → schedule agent will NOT count these days as busy.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with title like 'John in town', is_all_day=true, blocks_availability=false, dates jun 7-9. Renders as a non-blocking bar. Schedule agent still reports the artist as available those days.

evals.prompt: husband out of town next week
evals.context: Third-party informational event. Artist is still free — 'husband out of town' is a note, not a block. 'Next week' is a vague range; the model should still produce an end_date spanning the week.
evals.resolution: type=Personal, informational only. blocks_availability=false (third-party). Date/end_date should cover next week (exact days flexible — runner asserts informational flags, not exact range).
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with a title like 'Husband out of town', is_all_day=true, blocks_availability=false, spanning some portion of next week. Schedule agent remains unaffected.

evals.prompt: kids at camp next mon through fri
evals.context: Third-party informational event across a multi-day range. Must not block availability. 'mon through fri' is an inclusive range — end_date = midnight of saturday (exclusive-end).
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. 'kids' is NOT a client_name. Title something like 'Kids at camp'. blocks_availability=false — informational.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens as a non-blocking all-day event spanning next Mon-Fri. Artist still shows free for bookings those days.

evals.context: Single-day all-day blocking event. 'Day off' is explicitly artist-unavailable — strong blocking cue even though it's all-day (which normally defaults to informational).
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. 'day off' overrides the all-day default to blocks_availability=true — artist is unavailable.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with is_all_day=true, blocks_availability=true, single-day range may 20. Schedule agent treats may 20 as unavailable.

evals.context: Timed multi-day event. Even though 'tattoo' appears, 'convention' is a Personal calendar block (not a client session). Timed → blocks_availability defaults to true.
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. is_all_day=false because explicit times given. end_date = sunday 5pm ISO. Title like 'Tattoo Convention'. Timed Personal defaults to blocking.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with timed multi-day Personal, blocks_availability=true. MonthView renders bar across days; DayView renders timed endpoints.

evals.context: Ambiguous case: 'graduation' is a non-tattoo noun → Personal. No time given → is_all_day=true. Whether the artist has to attend is unclear. Default for ambiguous all-day Personal: blocks_availability=false (informational).
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. 'Lauren' is NOT a client (graduation context → relative/friend). Title keeps the name. is_all_day=true, blocks_availability defaults to false for ambiguous all-day.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with title='Lauren's Graduation', is_all_day=true, blocks_availability=false, single-day range. Artist can override blocking in the form.

evals.context: Timed single-day event (NOT multi-day, NOT all-day). Should NOT set end_date or is_all_day. blocks_availability defaults to true (timed Personal, artist-first).
evals.resolution: type=Personal → skip client resolution. Timed → is_all_day should NOT be set (or set to false). end_date should NOT be set for same-day timed events — duration handles that. blocks_availability default true but may be omitted if not explicit.
evals.expected_outcome: BookingForm opens with type=Personal, title=Dentist, date=may 12 10am, 1-hour default duration, NO all-day toggle, NO multi-day range.
